summary: Matched a small slice of the effect library by adding C for `efLib_8005CC2C` and `efLib_8005FCD8`, plus the effect-side forward/types scaffolding and baselib particle/AppSRT type corrections needed to express those functions. The PR also marked the corresponding scratches as OK and reformatted/updated the remaining `asm/melee/ef/eflib.s` with modern `.fn`/`.endfn` and `.obj` assembly annotations.
what_changed: Added `efLib_8005CC2C(int arg0, HSD_JObj* arg1)`, which calls `hsd_8039EFAC(0, arg0 / 1000, arg0, arg1)` and clears `PSAPPSRT_UNK_B10` on the returned particle/generator when non-null.

Added `efLib_8005FCD8(int arg0, int arg2, ef_UnkStruct1* arg3)`, which allocates an effect struct via `efLib_8005BE88(0)`, stores callback/data fields, creates a particle generator with `hsd_8039EFAC`, ensures a `UnkGeneratorMember` via `psAddGeneratorAppSRT_begin`, updates generator flags, and destroys the generator with `hsd_8039D4DC` if AppSRT creation fails.
Introduced `src/melee/ef/forward.h` with forward declarations for `ef_UnkStruct0`, `ef_UnkStruct1`, and `ef_UnkStruct2`.

Introduced `src/melee/ef/types.h` with provisional layouts for the effect structs used by the newly matched functions, including offset-labeled fields through `ef_UnkStruct0::x18` and `ef_UnkStruct1::xC`.
Updated `eflib.h` to include effect forward declarations and to add address-commented prototypes for `efLib_DestroyAll`, `efLib_PauseAll`, `efLib_ResumeAll`, `efLib_8005BE88`, and `fn_8005FBE4`.
Changed `hsd_8039EFAC` in `particle.h` from returning `void` to returning `UnkGeneratorStruct*`, enabling C to use the created generator directly.

Expanded `psappsrt.h` with AppSRT flag constants `PSAPPSRT_UNK_B09`, `PSAPPSRT_UNK_B10`, and `PSAPPSRT_UNK_B11`, and replaced an opaque two-word fill in `UnkGeneratorMember` with `UNK_T x0` and `UnkGeneratorStruct* x4`.
Added `UnkGeneratorMember` to baselib forward declarations and cleaned up forward declarations around `HSD_ImageDesc`/`HSD_CObjDesc`.
Renamed `struct _HSD_ImageDesc` to `struct HSD_ImageDesc` in `tobj.h` and updated associated pointer fields to use the non-underscore struct tag.

Updated `asm/melee/ef/eflib.s` heavily, including `.file "eflib.c"`, `.text`, `.balign 4`, `.fn`/`.endfn` function wrappers, `fn_8005....` labels for local callbacks, hexadecimal immediates, and `.obj`/`.float` object formatting.
smart_moves: Recovered just enough effect and baselib structure information to make the two target functions readable without over-naming unknown data beyond evidence; provisional fields remain offset-style (`x0`, `x4`, `x16_flags`, etc.).
Moved particle/AppSRT flag manipulation into named macros instead of open-coded bit numbers, making future particle effect work easier to read and less error-prone.
Corrected the return type of `hsd_8039EFAC` once the matched code proved callers use the returned `UnkGeneratorStruct*`.

Split effect forward declarations into `ef/forward.h` and concrete provisional layouts into `ef/types.h`, matching the project pattern of separating dependency-light forward headers from struct definitions.
Preserved uncertain callbacks/functions as address-based names like `fn_8005FBE4` rather than inventing semantic names.
decomp_lessons: For effect/particle code, matching may require first fixing baselib prototypes: a wrong `void` return on a factory-like function can block straightforward C for callers.
When a generator has optional AppSRT state, model the existing pointer (`x54`) and only call `psAddGeneratorAppSRT_begin` when it is null; this pattern appears central to `efLib_8005FCD8`.
Bitfield flag recovery can be introduced incrementally with neutral names (`PSAPPSRT_UNK_B09` etc.) when exact semantics are unknown but bit positions are proven by matching.
Use offset-based struct fields for newly recovered effect structs until stronger names are known; this keeps the match useful while avoiding misleading semantic guesses.
Scratch entries should be promoted from percentage/100% to `OK` when code is committed and matching in-tree.
Large asm-only diffs may include mechanical assembly normalization unrelated to the semantic C change; future readers should focus on the new C and supporting header deltas for intent.
naming_conventions: Effect library functions use `efLib_` plus address when unnamed, e.g. `efLib_8005CC2C` and `efLib_8005FCD8`.
Unknown local callbacks/functions use `fn_8005....` address names, e.g. `fn_8005FBE4`, rather than `.L_` labels when referenced as function pointers.
Provisional effect structs are named `ef_UnkStruct0`, `ef_UnkStruct1`, and `ef_UnkStruct2` and are forward-declared in `ef/forward.h`.
Unknown struct fields use offset-style names such as `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, `x10`, `x14`, and `x18`.
Recovered flag macros in `psappsrt.h` use the subsystem prefix and bit index form `PSAPPSRT_UNK_B09`, `PSAPPSRT_UNK_B10`, `PSAPPSRT_UNK_B11`.
Headers keep address comments on public prototypes, such as `/* 05B880 */ void efLib_DestroyAll(HSD_GObj* gobj);`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The matched C uses `arg0 / 1000` as the second argument to `hsd_8039EFAC`, matching the particle ID/category split visible in the recovered logic.
`efLib_8005FCD8` preserves the allocation/fail path: allocate effect struct, create generator, create/find AppSRT member, set flags if successful, otherwise call `hsd_8039D4DC`.
Assembly labels were modernized from raw `.global` labels to `.fn name, global` and `.endfn name`, improving function boundary metadata.
Referenced local code labels in assembly were renamed from `.L_8005....` to `fn_8005....` where they are used as function pointers/callbacks.
Data objects in assembly were expressed with `.obj`/`.endobj` and typed directives like `.float 0.1` instead of raw `.4byte` where possible.
Hex literal formatting was normalized in assembly comments/instructions, e.g. `li r5, 0x4` and `lwz r0, 0x8(r5)`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename `ef_UnkStruct0`, `ef_UnkStruct1`, or `ef_UnkStruct2` to semantic effect types.
Check later baselib particle work for semantic names of `PSAPPSRT_UNK_B09`, `PSAPPSRT_UNK_B10`, and `PSAPPSRT_UNK_B11`.
Search for additional callers of `hsd_8039EFAC` that depend on its `UnkGeneratorStruct*` return value.
Look for later PRs around `efLib_8005BE88` and `fn_8005FBE4` to understand the allocation/callback model used by `efLib_8005FCD8`.
Audit whether the `HSD_ImageDesc` struct tag cleanup in `tobj.h` was reused or further refined in later header-cleanup PRs.
